summary:
In Down syndrome, upper molars exhibit severe periodontitis primarily due to taurodontism, a condition involving enlarged pulp chambers and apical furcation shift that compromises tooth structure and promotes aggressive periodontal disease. This makes upper molars more vulnerable compared to other teeth or even lower molars.  

\boxed{C}

\boxed{C}. Answer choice: B
